eval_meta.about: Two-stage retrieval measure-first probe: bge dense (A) vs bge retrieve top-K + Core ML cross-encoder rerank (B). SYNTHETIC-but-representative (Claude-authored labels), directional build/no-build evidence, NOT ground-truth production data.
eval_meta.compute_units: ComputeUnit.ALL (ANE-eligible; latency is measured end-to-end, never a claim any op ran on the ANE)

metric_defs.recall@k: avg over queries of |relevant in top-k| / |relevant|
metric_defs.success@k: avg over queries of 1[any relevant in top-k]
metric_defs.MRR: mean reciprocal rank of the first relevant fact
metric_defs.nDCG@10: avg over queries of DCG@10 / ideal-DCG@10 (binary gain)
metric_defs.dense_recall@K: avg over queries of |relevant in dense top-K| / |relevant| — the ceiling a rerank of depth K can recover to (rerank reorders, never retrieves)

A_dense.name: bge dense cosine over all facts (shipped behavior)

verdict.call: GO: the cross-encoder rerank MEASURABLY improves ranking (best at K=20); ship it config-gated on top of the bge bi-encoder as the standard two-stage stack.
verdict.honesty: SYNTHETIC-but-representative eval, directional evidence not a production guarantee. recall@k (k<=K) after rerank is bounded by dense_recall@K. Latency is measured end-to-end under ComputeUnit.ALL; ANE-eligible, never claimed to run on the ANE.
